data.name: Human Germline Editing Moratorium
data.description: International consensus against heritable human genome editing until safety and societal implications are resolved. He Jiankui's 2018 CRISPR babies violated this consensus.
data.controversy: Potential to eliminate genetic diseases vs. risk of eugenics, unintended consequences, and inequality
data.regulation: 73 countries ban or restrict heritable genome editing; WHO global governance framework (2021)

data.name: Synthetic Biology Biosecurity
data.description: The same tools that enable beneficial synthetic biology (DNA synthesis, gene editing) could be used to create harmful pathogens. DNA screening and biosecurity frameworks are evolving.
data.controversy: Open science vs. security; democratization of biotech increases both innovation and risk
data.regulation: US DNA synthesis screening guidelines (2024); HHS Tier 1 select agent oversight; IBBS international biosecurity

data.name: Longevity Inequality
data.description: If anti-aging therapies work, they may initially be available only to the wealthy, exacerbating health and lifespan inequality.
data.controversy: Life extension as a human right vs. luxury good; who pays for longevity treatments?
data.regulation: No specific regulation; Medicare/insurance coverage debates will intensify as therapies emerge
